title: TD Garden owner sues cannabis dispensary The Boston Garden
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Delaware North is suing The Boston Garden dispensary for allegedly infringing on its trademark linked to the famous sports arena.
The Boston Garden dispensary, co-founded by actor Jonathan Tucker, operates locations in Athol, Cambridge, and Somerville.
Delaware North claims the dispensary's name could confuse the public regarding commercial affiliation with the TD Garden arena.

title: Former Quincy official pleads guilty to embezzling city funds for 153 pounds of steak tips, studio recordings, framed self-portrait
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Thomas Clasby Jr., former Quincy official, pleaded guilty to charges including embezzlement and wire fraud in Boston federal court.
Clasby used city funds for personal purchases, including 153 pounds of steak tips and music studio time, as per prosecutors.
Sentencing is set for June 17, with possible penalties including fines up to $250,000 and 20 years in prison.

title: ‘We do feel like we’re being invaded’: Nantucket agrees to try restricted hours at popular tourist destination
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Nantucket to trial restricted hours on popular Sconset Bluff Walk due to privacy concerns from local residents.
The Select Board voted to limit walk hours from 8:30 a.m. to 7:30 p.m., replacing previous 7:30 a.m. to sunset.
Siasconset Civic Association plans to hire a docent for visitor education during peak summer months.

title: Queue these up: What to watch on Netflix and more in March
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Netflix adds 'The Lego Movie' and 'Misery' to its streaming library in March 2026.
'The Secret Agent', an Oscar-nominated film set in Brazil, is now available on Hulu.
Boston.com staff recommends shows like 'A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms' and 'Wonder Man'.

title: Why he’s running the Boston Marathon in memory of family treated at Dana-Farber
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Trevor Murray, 30, from Malden, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for Dana-Farber Cancer Institute.
Murray runs in memory of his father, Jeff, and sister-in-law, Grace, treated at Dana-Farber before passing in 2020.
Jeff Murray previously ran the marathon in 2018 while undergoing cancer treatment, inspiring Trevor's participation.

title: MBTA announces schedule changes, detours as nearly 1 million expected to attend Sunday’s St. Patrick’s Day parade
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: MBTA plans schedule changes, detours for St. Patrick's Day parade in South Boston, expecting nearly 1 million attendees.
Red Line offers rush hour service from 10 a.m. to 6 p.m., may bypass Broadway stop due to heavy crowds.
Shuttle buses will run from South Station to South Boston; multiple bus routes to face suspensions and detours.

title: Boston Public Schools graduation rate reached all-time high last year, still lags behind state average
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Boston Public Schools' graduation rate reached a record high of 81.3% in 2025, up 1.6% from 2024.
The district's dropout rate fell to 3.6%, marking the lowest on record, excluding the pandemic-affected 2021.
Despite improvements, Boston's graduation rate remains 8 percentage points below the state average of 89.3%.

title: Bruins’ playoff hopes are destined to fail if they can’t solve confounding road woes
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins have an 11-14-5 road record, jeopardizing their playoff prospects despite a strong home performance.
Don Sweeney avoided major trades, opting to rely on the current squad aiming for postseason success.
Boston holds the second wild-card spot with 76 points, but must improve road performance to secure playoffs.

title: This Marblehead man is running the Boston Marathon for his pediatric patients
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Brian Wishart, 42, from Marblehead, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support pediatric patients at Spaulding Rehab.
Wishart, Chief of Pediatric Rehabilitation Medicine, aims to integrate Spaulding's research into clinical care for children's treatment.
Funds raised will support adaptive sports programs and resources, enhancing care for all pediatric patients, regardless of background.

title: 8 children have now died from flu in Mass. this season
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Eight children and 298 adults have died from the flu in Massachusetts this season, per state health data.
Massachusetts flu vaccination rate is 38.1% as of Feb. 28, while flu-like activity was moderate in late February.
Health officials urge flu vaccination for everyone over six months, citing risks particularly for children under two.
